title: Control owner attestation freshness
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate individual test outcomes (covered by 22.47.1).
evidence: Saved search 'owner_attestation_freshness' (weekly) emits controls whose owner attestation is older than cadence.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CC1.3 management's commitment to a culture of control includes owner attestation.
CA-2 control assessments and owner attestations.
A.5.35 independent review of information security.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Control AC-2 has an owner attestation from 14 months ago (cadence: 12 months); the UC fires.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Control attestation within 12 months; no alert.

spl:
| inputlookup control_inventory.csv
| lookup owner_attestations.csv control_id OUTPUT last_attested signed_by
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(last_attested,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where age_days>attestation_cadence_days OR isnull(last_attested)
| table control_id owner last_attested age_days attestation_cadence_days
description: Elevates owner attestation from an email thread to a tracked GRC metric. Owner attestation freshness is a bedrock SOC 2 CC1.3 criterion.
value: Delivers the internal-audit team a continuous view of 'who has signed what'.
implementation: (1) Emit owner_attestations.csv from GRC workflow; (2) run weekly.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: GRC owner attestation ledger, control_inventory.csv..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Emit owner_attestations.csv from GRC workflow; (2) run weekly.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup control_inventory.csv
| lookup owner_attestations.csv control_id OUTPUT last_attested signed_by
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(last_attested,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where age_days>attestation_cadence_days OR isnull(last_attested)
| table control_id owner last_attested age_days attestation_cadence_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**Control owner attestation freshness** — Delivers the internal-audit team a continuous view of 'who has signed what'.

Documented **Data sources**: GRC owner attestation ledger, control_inventory.csv. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where age_days>attestation_cadence_days OR isnull(last_attested)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Control owner attestation freshness**): table control_id owner last_attested age_days attestation_cadence_days


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heatmap of control × age buckets, table of overdue controls, single-value '% of controls with fresh attestation'.
visualization: Heatmap of control × age buckets, table of overdue controls, single-value '% of controls with fresh attestation'.
knownFalsePositives: New controls in onboarding need a grace window.
